content.text:
**MDL module name:** `contract_nli_no_align_dev_13_nda_16`

```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement (NDA) – 11 Aug 2015 (PJ M & Transmission Owner)
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_13_nda_16

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties to the Agreement
type Party = {
    name: string,               # e.g. "PJM" or "Transmission Owner"
    is_disclosing: bool,        # true if this Party supplies Confidential Information
    is_receiving: bool          # true if this Party receives Confidential Information
}

@ Confidential Information that is exchanged under the NDA
type ConfidentialInformation = {
    id: int,                    # unique identifier for the piece of information
    owner: Party,               # the Disclosing Party that supplied it
    holder: Party,              # the Receiving Party that currently possesses it
    is_returned: bool,          # true when the information has been returned
    is_destroyed: bool          # true when the information has been destroyed
}

@ The NDA itself – we only need the termination flag for this model
type Agreement = {
    terminated: bool            # true when the Agreement has been terminated
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities (public model objects)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The NDA that governs the relationship
entity agreement: Agreement

@ The Party that is receiving Confidential Information (the “Receiving Party”)
entity receiving_party: Party

@ A piece of Confidential Information that the Receiving Party holds
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInformation

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – deontic obligations extracted from the NDA
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 8 – “Ownership and Use of Confidential Information”
@ The Receiving Party must return or destroy Confidential Information
@ when the Agreement is terminated.
rule O return_or_destroy_on_termination:
    (agreement.terminated implies
        (confidential_info.is_returned or confidential_info.is_destroyed)) always

@ Clause 2 – “Reviewing Representative”
@ A Reviewing Representative (a member of the Receiving Party) may only
@ have access if they have executed a Non‑Disclosure Certificate.
rule O reviewing_rep_must_have_certificate:
    receiving_party.is_receiving implies
        (confidential_info.holder = receiving_party) always

@ Clause 5 – “Non‑Disclosure to Third Parties”
@ No Confidential Information may be disclosed to a third party
@ unless the Disclosing Party gives prior written approval.
rule F disclose_to_third_party_without_approval:
    (confidential_info.is_returned or confidential_info.is_destroyed) implies
        (confidential_info.holder.is_disclosing) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions (optional – keep the rules readable)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
func is_terminated(a: Agreement) -> bool: a.terminated

func is_returned_or_destroyed(ci: ConfidentialInformation) -> bool:
    ci.is_returned or ci.is_destroyed
```

**Explanation of the hypothesis**

The hypothesis *“Receiving Party shall destroy or return some Confidential Information upon the termination of Agreement.”* is captured by the rule `return_or_destroy_on_termination`.  
- The antecedent `agreement.terminated` models the moment the Agreement ends.  
- The consequent `confidential_info.is_returned or confidential_info.is_destroyed` expresses the obligation to either return **or** destroy the information.  
- The `always` temporal operator makes the obligation hold at every step of the trace where the antecedent is true, i.e., from the point of termination onward.  

If the solver finds a trace where `agreement.terminated` is true but both `confidential_info.is_returned` and `confidential_info.is_destroyed` are false, the rule will be violated and the model will be reported as **unsat**, confirming that the hypothesis is a required obligation under the NDA.
